description: Evaluates the user representative skill across different deliverable types, audience personas, and quality dimensions. Each eval targets a specific review capability or scoring behavior.

evals.name: executive-deliverable-jargon-heavy
evals.prompt: Review for clarity: 'The proposed architecture leverages event-driven microservices with CQRS and saga orchestration patterns, achieving eventual consistency through CDC pipelines with Debezium connectors feeding into Kafka Streams for materialized view hydration.' Target audience: executives.
evals.expected_output: Low comprehension score for executive audience. Multiple micro-adjustments to replace jargon. Bias flags for technical bias (assuming reader knows distributed systems).
expectations: Comprehension score below 7 for executive audience (heavy jargon without explanation)
Identifies at least 5 unexplained technical terms (CQRS, saga, CDC, Debezium, Kafka Streams, materialized view)
Micro-adjustments propose plain language alternatives with technical definitions
Technical bias flagged: assumes reader knows distributed systems architecture
Verdict is CONDITIONAL or FAIL for executive audience
Executive persona applied: 5-minute scan, zero jargon tolerance

evals.name: well-structured-developer-doc
evals.prompt: Evaluate from user perspective: a developer-facing API documentation with code examples, clear headers, request/response samples, and error handling guide. Audience: developers.
evals.expected_output: High scores across dimensions for developer audience. Minimal micro-adjustments. Verdict: PASS.
expectations: Comprehension score >= 7 for developer audience (technical terms acceptable)
Cognitive load score reflects good structure (clear headers, chunked sections)
Accessibility score reflects code examples and request/response samples
Adoption risks are minimal for developer audience
Verdict is PASS (all scores >= 7)
Developer persona applied: 30-minute deep dive, high jargon tolerance

evals.name: multi-audience-document
evals.prompt: Check readability of a 25-page architecture decision document that will be read by executives, technical leads, and developers.
evals.expected_output: Multi-audience edge case: recommends reader track structure (exec summary > technical detail > appendix). Very long document triggers TOC requirement.
expectations: All 4 reader personas applied (executive, technical lead, developer, business analyst)
Recommends 'reader track' structure: exec summary > technical detail > appendix
Very long document (>20 pages) triggers requirement for TOC + section summaries + key takeaway boxes
Accessibility score evaluates 80/20 rule: can reader get 80% value in 20% reading time
Different scores per persona (exec may score lower than developer on same content)
Navigation micro-adjustments proposed (section links, jump-to-recommendations)

evals.name: optimism-bias-detection
evals.prompt: Assess adoption risk for a project proposal that claims 'migration will be seamless with zero downtime, no additional training needed, and immediate 50% cost reduction from day one.'
evals.expected_output: Optimism bias flagged on multiple claims. Adoption risk assessment identifies stakeholder skepticism potential. Bias flags include overstated benefits and underplayed risks.
expectations: Optimism bias detected: 'seamless', 'zero downtime', 'no training needed', 'immediate 50% reduction'
Each optimistic claim flagged with specific bias description and suggested fix
Adoption risk identifies stakeholder skepticism when claims seem too good to be true
Micro-adjustments propose realistic language (ranges, conditions, timelines)
Does not just flag bias but provides concrete rewrite suggestions
Organizational bias may also be flagged (assuming org can absorb change without training)

evals.name: cognitive-load-overloaded-table
evals.prompt: Check cognitive load on a deliverable containing a single table with 15 columns and 50 rows of technical metrics, no summary, no callout boxes, and no section headers.
evals.expected_output: Very low cognitive load score. Micro-adjustments: split table, add key insight callout, add section headers, add TL;DR summary.
expectations: Cognitive load score below 5 (information overload, no chunking)
Accessibility score low (no way to get 80% value in 20% reading time)
Micro-adjustment: split 15-column table into 2-3 focused tables
Micro-adjustment: add 'key insight' callout above the table
Micro-adjustment: add section headers and TL;DR summary
Verdict is FAIL (scores below 5 require rework)
Simplification type micro-adjustment: move non-essential columns to appendix
